fund.name: Franklin India SHORT TERM INCOME PLAN - Direct - GROWTH

allocations.rationale: Since your risk appetite is conservative and goals are short-term (medical emergency), this fund appeals to debt instruments (7–8% returns with 99.9% monthly stability). Use it for stability and to transition from FDs without sacrificing returns.

fund.name: Invesco India Multicap Fund - Direct Plan - Growth Option

allocations.rationale: Multi-cap funds invest in large, mid, and small companies and have been shown to outperform large-cap funds over long horizons (3–5 years). With a 10-year horizon, this provides diversification and is a steady way to build your equity exposure.

Gold doesn't move 1:1 with stocks and is a great shield against inflation. Over 10 years, gold ETFs have returned ~25% CAGR on average; it's your "insurance" portfolio. The tax treatment is slab-based: your gains will be taxed at your 20% slab.

fund.name: Motilal Oswal Large and Midcap Fund - Direct Plan Growth

allocations.rationale: This fund has 3-year CAGR of 26.9% (highest in its category) driven by careful stock-picking. With a 10-year horizon, large and mid-cap funds help capture growth while keeping risk in check — mid-caps are risky alone, but this blend is balanced.

fund.name: HDFC Mid Cap Opportunity Fund? Wait! Let's check...

allocations.rationale: Wait, let me get the details for HDFC Mid Cap Fund and correct this in the final plan! It has a 3-year CAGR of 25.0% and has been a popular choice for medium-term growth.

plan.setup_phase: Step 1: Open a direct mutual fund account (free and no commission) on Groww, Zerodha Coin, or Kuvera. Step 2: Set up a monthly SIP for HDFC Mid Cap Fund (₹3,100), Motilal Oswal Large & Mid (₹7,750), Franklin Short TM Debt (₹10,850), Gold MF (₹3,100), and Invesco Multicap (₹6,200). Step 3: Once your FDs mature, park the proceeds in the same SIPs or as a lump-sum top-up of no more than 10–15% of your corpus to avoid timing risk.
review_checkpoints: After 6 months: Confirm all SIPs are running and AICR values haven't changed. If any SIP has stopped, contact your bank/AMC.

After 1 year: Check the actual returns. For short-term debt, aim for 7–8% returns (if you're seeing less, consider moving to an overnight fund). For equity funds, compare against the category average — if FD Stock Midcap has lagged for 2 years straight, switch to a better performer.
After 3 years: Rebalance your portfolio — shift 2–4% of equity exposure to debt if your equity has grown 10%+ more than planned or if the market has hit a major high.
plan.rebalancing_guidelines: Once a year (say, on your birthday), review your portfolio. If your equity portion has grown to 40% (18% higher than your target), move 5% of your investments from large/mid-cap or multi-cap to short-term debt or gold. This is how you stay disciplined and avoid 'chasing highs' or 'selling low'.
plan.rationale: You're 51 with a 20-year retirement left (assuming retirement at 65). Your goals (medical emergency + preserve wealth) lean toward safety, but since your horizon is 10 years, equity can fight inflation better than FDs. We've kept 35% in debt for stability and added 10% gold as insurance. The rest goes into diversified equity funds that have outperformed indices. This plan aims for ~12.3% CAGR over 10 years — enough to beat inflation (6–7%) and build your corpus while preserving it.
risks: Equity funds can drop 20–30% in a bad year (like 2008 or 2020). If you panic sell, you'll lock in losses. Stick to your SIPs and live long enough to ride out the waves.
Debt funds are "safer" but not guaranteed — short-duration funds are stable like FDs, but long-duration funds can be affected by interest rate hikes. We've picked short-term: stable, predictable.

Gold isn't expected to outpace stocks 100% of the time. If prices rise, great; if they fall, it may be a good time to sip in more. Over 10 years, gold's purpose is inflation protection, not wealth explosion.
Tax risk: Debt gains are taxed at your 20% slab. If tax rates rise, your post-tax returns will fall. Equity gains are taxed at 12.5% after 1 year, which is better.
plan.disclaimer: For research/educational purposes only. Not certified financial advice.

sip_step_up.description: Increase each SIP by 10% every year to match salary growth (assuming 10% annual salary growth).

allocation_schedule.trigger: If equity has grown 10%+ above baseline or mid-cap lags category for 2 quarters.
If you have 50%+ of your 30L goal achieved or want to lock more money.
